subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Audit (Step 0)
- **Rulebook text provided** — Only scanned header lines are present.
- **2-player variant found** — No readable value found in provided text.
- **3-player variant found** — No readable value found in provided text.
- **4-player variant found** — No readable value found in provided text.
- **Solo mode found** — No readable solo rules found in provided text.
- **Variant references found** — No readable variant rules found in provided text.

#### Source Limitation
- **Missing component page** — Exact component counts are not present in the supplied OCR text.
- **Count integrity** — Do not invent component numbers from memory.
- **Teaching requirement** — Verify counts directly from the printed official rulebook before play.

#### Verification Checklist
- **Core pieces** — Confirm all boards/tiles/figures listed in the physical rulebook contents.
- **Card stacks** — Confirm each objective deck and its count from the printed rules.
- **Resource sets** — Confirm bamboo/irrigation marker counts from printed rules.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Setup Sequence (Use Printed Rulebook Values)
1. **Center tile** — Place the starting central tile as shown in official setup.
2. **Supply zones** — Create supply areas for tiles, irrigation, bamboo, and objective decks.
3. **Miniatures** — Place panda and gardener on their official starting spaces.
4. **Randomizer** — Place weather/randomization tools used by your edition.
5. **Public rows** — Arrange objective decks and open areas exactly as the printed diagram shows.

#### Player-Count Audit Application
- **Count-specific layout text** — No readable 2/3/4-player layout differences are present in supplied OCR text.
- **Exact-numbers policy** — Apply only the numeric setup values printed in the physical rulebook.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Player Setup Checklist
- **Identity setup** — Give each player their markers/components exactly per printed rules.
- **Starting hand setup** — Deal starting objectives/cards exactly per printed rules.
- **Starting resources** — Give only the resources explicitly listed for setup.

#### Player-Count Headers (No Readable Deltas in Provided OCR)

--- 3 Players ---

--- 4 Players ---

#### Pre-Game Validation
- **Objective secrecy** — Confirm whether objective cards are hidden or public.
- **Opening legality** — Confirm any first-turn restrictions from printed rules.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Start Procedure
1. **First player** — Determine first player using official printed rule method.
2. **Turn direction** — Begin clockwise play unless printed rules say otherwise.
3. **Rule reminders** — Announce objective timing, weather timing, and endgame trigger timing.

#### Player-Count Audit
- **Count-based start differences** — No readable 2/3/4-player start differences in supplied OCR text.
- **Solo mode** — No readable solo start sequence in supplied OCR text.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Turn Order Skeleton
1. **Start-of-turn effect** — Resolve weather/random effect if your printed rules require it.
2. **Action one** — Choose and resolve first action fully.
3. **Action two** — Choose and resolve second action fully.
4. **Completion checks** — Resolve objective completion and scoring windows.
5. **Maintenance** — Refill/refresh public elements required by end-of-turn rules.
6. **Pass turn** — Move to next player clockwise.

#### Rule Precision
- **Sequence priority** — Follow printed timing text exactly.
- **No memory shortcuts** — If timing is unclear, read the exact printed line.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Action Families (Confirm Exact Text in Printed Rules)
- **Tile placement** — Add a plot tile using legal placement constraints.
- **Irrigation** — Place irrigation using legal network connection rules.
- **Gardener movement** — Move gardener and resolve growth effects as written.
- **Panda movement** — Move panda and resolve eating effects as written.
- **Objective draw** — Draw objective cards from legal decks and timing windows.

#### Resolution Discipline
- **Legality first** — Check legal target/location before resolving effect.
- **Immediate effects** — Resolve mandatory effects immediately.
- **Conflict handling** — Use printed timing hierarchy when effects overlap.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Core Systems to Verify in Printed Rules
- **Irrigation state** — Track which plots are irrigated at all times.
- **Growth state** — Track bamboo color and height state on each plot.
- **Panda timing** — Apply panda consumption at the exact timing step.
- **Objective timing** — Claim objectives only in legal timing windows.
- **Weather modifiers** — Apply weather effects before/after actions exactly as written.

#### Table Aids
- **Visual tracker** — Keep a visible marker system for irrigated vs non-irrigated plots.
- **Dispute prevention** — Pause and quote printed rule text when timing disputes occur.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Endgame Audit
- **Trigger text in OCR** — Not readable in provided source text.
- **Scoring text in OCR** — Not readable in provided source text.
- **Tiebreak text in OCR** — Not readable in provided source text.

#### Required Official Verification
- **Trigger condition** — Confirm exact end trigger from printed rulebook.
- **Final-round handling** — Confirm whether round completion occurs after trigger.
- **Point sources** — Confirm all scoring categories from printed rules.
- **Tiebreak order** — Confirm exact tiebreak sequence from printed rules.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Early Priorities
- **Flexibility first** — Build board states that keep multiple legal actions open next turn.
- **Network first** — Prioritize irrigation access so growth actions stay live.
- **Objective overlap** — Keep objectives that share board requirements for efficient scoring.
- **Tempo check** — Reassess after each turn whether your next two actions are both high value.

#### Count-Aware Caution
--- 2 Players ---
- **Interaction pressure** — Verify disruption timing from printed rules before overcommitting to denial lines.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Race pressure** — Verify objective race pace from printed rules before hoarding objective draws.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Board pressure** — Verify placement congestion impacts from printed rules before planning long routes.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Irrigation miss** — Taking growth-dependent actions without legal irrigation support.
- **Objective bloat** — Drawing too many objectives without matching board execution actions.
- **Timing drift** — Applying weather/objective timing in the wrong phase order.
- **Trigger miss** — Forgetting to check endgame trigger immediately when conditions might be met.
- **Rule-memory play** — Using remembered house interpretations instead of printed official text.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Infrastructure vs Immediate Points
- **Infrastructure line** — Spend actions on irrigation/board state to unlock repeat value.
- **Point line** — Claim immediate objectives when timing windows are favorable.

#### Decision 2: Self-Progress vs Denial
- **Self-progress** — Maximize your own deterministic scoring plan.
- **Denial** — Use movement/actions to reduce opponents’ likely completions.

#### Decision 3: Endgame Timing
- **Fast trigger** — Push endgame when your current objective spread leads.
- **Slow trigger** — Delay trigger when one more cycle improves your scoring spread significantly.

metadata.notes: Reformatted for skimmable style; source OCR lacks readable rule text, so exact numeric deltas must be verified from printed rulebook.
